thinking:
**Initiating Planning Phase**

Okay, the scratch directory is empty, which is ideal. I've initiated a plan, but I'm pausing it now. It's time to check the instructions for Planning Mode; the task says to thoroughly research using research tools before making any changes. This is good: I'll stick to research for now.


**Drafting Implementation Steps**

I'm now drafting the implementation plan. I've decided on the core files: `index.html`, `style.css`, and `app.js`. For the UI/UX, I'm thinking of a sleek header with theme toggling and progress tracking. The todo form will feature auto-focus and category/priority tagging. I'll need to figure out details for the data-persisting logic. My next step is structuring the `implementation_plan.md` artifact. I will set `request_feedback` to `true` in `ArtifactMetadata`.


**Refining Feature Design**

I'm now refining the design of the features. The idea is for each todo item to have a slide-in animation on add. I'm focusing on the implementation details for inline editing. I've got detailed design ideas for each todo item. Completed items will have a visual completed state, with an easily accessible delete flow. I'm thinking of a "Clear Completed Tasks" button, along with a prominent delete option for each completed task. I need to make sure delete actions are clear and intuitive with a nice animation.

args.ArtifactMetadata: {"ArtifactType":"implementation_plan","RequestFeedback":true,"Summary":"Implementation plan for creating a beautiful, premium, single-page Todo List web application in the scratch/todo-app directory, featuring task creation, editing, status toggling, categories, priority tags, local storage persistence, and dynamic animations."}
args.CodeContent:
"# Todo List Application - Implementation Plan

We will create a visually stunning, responsive, and feature-rich Single Page Application (SPA) for a Todo List in `C:\Users\lalkr\.gemini\antigravity-ide\scratch\todo-app`. This app will support creating, updating, completing, and deleting tasks, and will use local storage to persist data.

## User Review Required

> [!IMPORTANT]
> Since you currently do not have an active workspace open, we will create this project in `C:\Users\lalkr\.gemini\antigravity-ide\scratch\todo-app`.
> Once created, **we highly recommend opening this directory (`C:\Users\lalkr\.gemini\antigravity-ide\scratch\todo-app`) as your active workspace in your IDE** for the best development experience.

> [!TIP]
> We will design the application with a sleek glassmorphism theme, offering:
> - Light/Dark mode toggling.
> - Task management with categories (Work, Personal, Shopping, etc.) and priority tags (High, Medium, Low).
> - Due dates with visual indicators.
> - Progress tracking panel showing total, pending, and completed counts with a animated progress bar.
> - Smooth drag/drop transitions or micro-animations for adding, checking, and deleting tasks.
> - Inline editing to easily update any part of a todo.

## Open Questions
- **Delete behavior**: Should completed tasks be automatically deleted, or should they remain in the list as "Completed" with an option to manually delete them? We propose keeping them in a completed state (strikethrough, faded style) with an explicit, easy-to-use delete button, plus a "Clear All Completed" button. Please let us know if you prefer auto-deletion instead.

## Proposed Changes

We will create the following files in `C:\Users\lalkr\.gemini\antigravity-ide\scratch\todo-app/`:

---

### Frontend Files

#### [NEW] [index.html](file:///C:/Users/lalkr/.gemini/antigravity-ide/scratch/todo-app/index.html)
- Main HTML structure.
- Modern fonts (Inter and Outfit) loaded from Google Fonts.
- FontAwesome icons fo

**Designing CSS Structure**

I'm now focusing on the CSS for the to-do app. My objective is to create a high-quality styling system. I'm starting with color palettes and themes. I've begun with a dark theme using deep purples, blues, and blacks.

I've been working on refining the color palettes, including both a dark and light theme, with particular attention to how colors harmonize. I'm also planning the glassmorphism elements, backdrop filters, and semi-transparent backgrounds.

thinking:
**Defining Core Application Structure**

Okay, I'm now focusing on outlining the core JavaScript application structure within `app.js`. My current thought process involves state management with a `todos` array and considering initial rendering strategies alongside event listener bindings. I also plan to define functions for saving and loading data from `localStorage`.


**Outlining UI Interaction Functions**

I'm now outlining key UI interaction functions within the application. I've defined `renderTodos()` to handle dynamic DOM element creation based on filters and search, including overdue flags. Also, I've created the `addTodo()` to process form inputs and assign unique IDs, priorities, due dates etc. I'm working on the logic for completing tasks with `toggleTodoComplete()`, and the removal of tasks with animations in `deleteTodo()`. I'm going to start on the modal edit functions soon, `openEditModal()`, `closeEditModal()`, and `updateTodo()`, and batch removing functionality with `clearCompleted()`. Finally, I'll need to create the `updateStats()` function to keep the dashboard up-to-date with total, pending, and completed tasks.
